title: Closed Body Shape at Kickoff
whatHappened: Ava starts the game with her back partially to the opponent's goal.
whyItMatters: Limits her ability to see forward passing lanes immediately.
microCorrection: Position the back foot at a 45-degree angle toward the target area.
u10Adaptation: Pretend you are a flashlight; shine your light on the whole field.

title: Lack of Width on Kickoff
whatHappened: The wingers are standing inside the center circle rather than stretching the pitch.
whyItMatters: Compresses the playing area, making it easier for the Red team to press.
microCorrection: Wide players should stay within 2 yards of the touchline during buildup.
u10Adaptation: Make the field as big as a giant's house.

title: Static Starting Positions
whatHappened: Players are standing flat-footed waiting for the first pass.
whyItMatters: Slows down the reaction time to the ball and opponent movements.
microCorrection: Keep weight on the balls of the feet with knees slightly bent.
u10Adaptation: Dance like you're on hot coals.

title: Forced Long Ball
whatHappened: Red team kicks the ball long into a crowded area immediately.
whyItMatters: Results in a 50/50 turnover rather than controlled possession.
microCorrection: Look for the short pass to the feet of a teammate first.
u10Adaptation: Don't just kick it; pass it to a teammate's toes.

title: Defensive Line Depth
whatHappened: Navy defenders are caught in a flat line as the long ball is played.
whyItMatters: One pass beats the entire defense, leading to a breakaway risk.
microCorrection: One defender should drop 5 yards deeper to act as a 'sweeper'.
u10Adaptation: One player stays back like a safety net.

title: Goalkeeper Hesitation
whatHappened: The Navy keeper waits for the ball to enter the box rather than meeting it early.
whyItMatters: Allows the attacker more time to reach the ball and shoot.
coachingCue: Be the boss of the box.
microCorrection: Attack the ball at the earliest possible point outside the 6-yard box.
u10Adaptation: Go get the ball before the 'monster' gets it.

title: Incomplete Clearance
whatHappened: The clearance from the Navy defender is sliced and stays in the central danger zone.
whyItMatters: Keeps the pressure on the defense and allows for a second-chance shot.
microCorrection: Lock the ankle and strike through the middle of the ball toward the sidelines.
u10Adaptation: Kick it to the trees, not the middle.

title: Recovery Sprint Speed
whatHappened: Midfielders are jogging back while the ball is in their defensive third.
whyItMatters: Leaves the defenders outnumbered in the box.
microCorrection: High-intensity 10-yard burst to get goal-side of the opponent.
u10Adaptation: Race your shadow back to the goal.

title: Bunching in Midfield
whatHappened: Four Navy players are within a 3-yard radius of each other.
whyItMatters: Eliminates passing options and clogs the dribbling lanes.
microCorrection: Maintain a minimum of 8-10 yards between teammates in the middle third.
u10Adaptation: Don't be a magnet; push away from your friends.

title: Throw-in Technique
whatHappened: The throw-in is executed with a slight foot lift (hard to see, but suspected).
whyItMatters: Results in a foul throw and loss of possession.
microCorrection: Drag the back toe to ensure contact with the ground remains.
u10Adaptation: Keep your feet stuck in the mud.

title: Receiving Under Pressure
whatHappened: Ava receives the ball and immediately turns into two defenders.
whyItMatters: High risk of turnover in a transition moment.
microCorrection: Scan before the ball arrives to identify the 'weak' side of the press.
u10Adaptation: Look for the 'ghosts' behind you before you touch the ball.

title: First Touch Direction
whatHappened: Ava's first touch stops the ball dead under her feet.
whyItMatters: Requires a second touch to get the ball moving, allowing defenders to close in.
microCorrection: Use the inside of the foot to push the ball 2 yards away from the body.
u10Adaptation: Push the ball out of your feet so you can run.

title: Upper Body Balance
whatHappened: Ava is leaning too far back while trying to shield the ball.
whyItMatters: Makes her easy to knock off balance by a smaller defender.
microCorrection: Lower the center of gravity by bending the knees and using an arm for feel.
u10Adaptation: Be like a sturdy table, not a tall tree.

title: Support Angles
whatHappened: Teammates are standing behind defenders, hiding from the ball carrier.
whyItMatters: Ava has no 'escape' pass, forcing her into a difficult 1v3.
coachingCue: Be a window, not a wall.
microCorrection: Move into the 'passing lanes' where you can see both the ball and the goal.
u10Adaptation: Hide-and-seek is for recess, not soccer; let your friend see you.

title: Scanning Before the Break
whatHappened: Ava wins the ball but keeps her head down for three full seconds.
whyItMatters: Misses the opportunity to see a teammate running wide left.
microCorrection: Lift the chin between every two touches of the ball.
u10Adaptation: Take a picture of the field with your eyes.

title: Explosive Acceleration
whatHappened: Ava recognizes the space and accelerates past the midfield line.
whyItMatters: Creates the numerical advantage needed for the counter-attack.
microCorrection: Drive the arms to generate more power in the first three steps.
u10Adaptation: Turn on your rocket boosters.

title: Dribbling with Laces
whatHappened: Ava uses the inside of her foot to dribble at high speed.
whyItMatters: Inside-foot dribbling is slower and breaks the running stride.
microCorrection: Use the 'pinky toe' area (outside/laces) to push the ball forward while running.
u10Adaptation: Run like a sprinter, not a duck.

title: Attacking the Space
whatHappened: Ava chooses to drive centrally toward the goal.
whyItMatters: The most direct path to scoring, putting the keeper under pressure.
microCorrection: Attack the 'near post' space to force the keeper to move.
u10Adaptation: Drive the car straight to the garage.

title: Agility in the Turn
whatHappened: A Navy teammate makes a sharp turn to avoid a collision.
whyItMatters: Prevents injury and maintains the flow of the attack.
microCorrection: Small, choppy steps to adjust body weight before a change of direction.
u10Adaptation: Be like a ninja, light on your feet.

title: Plant Foot Placement
whatHappened: Ava's plant foot appears to be too far behind the ball during the shot.
whyItMatters: Causes the ball to rise or lose power; reduces accuracy.
microCorrection: The plant foot should be parallel to the ball, pointing at the target.
u10Adaptation: Put your 'aiming foot' right next to the ball's house.

title: Shooting with Laces
whatHappened: Ava strikes the ball well with her laces for the goal.
whyItMatters: Provides the necessary power to beat the goalkeeper from distance.
microCorrection: Strike the center of the ball with the 'sweet spot' of the laces.
u10Adaptation: Hit it with your shoelaces, not your toes.

title: Shot Selection
whatHappened: Ava shoots early rather than trying to dribble around the keeper.
whyItMatters: Catches the keeper off guard before they can set their feet.
coachingCue: See the gap, hit the gap.
microCorrection: Aim for the corners of the goal, away from the keeper's reach.
u10Adaptation: Find the 'empty' part of the net.

title: Goalkeeper Positioning
whatHappened: The Red keeper is standing on the goal line during the shot.
whyItMatters: Gives the shooter the maximum angle to score.
microCorrection: Move 2-3 yards off the line to narrow the shooting window.
u10Adaptation: Come out and make yourself look like a giant wall.

title: Celebration Energy
whatHappened: The team celebrates with high intensity.
whyItMatters: Builds morale and team chemistry, which is vital at U10.
microCorrection: Ensure players quickly return to their positions to stay focused.
u10Adaptation: High fives for everyone, then back to work!

title: Post-Goal Organization
whatHappened: Navy players are slow to return to their half for the restart.
whyItMatters: Allows the opponent to catch them off guard if the ref blows the whistle early.
microCorrection: Immediately jog back to the starting formation after the celebration.
u10Adaptation: Get back to your 'home' spots quickly.

title: Midfield Tracking
whatHappened: Navy #12 watches the ball instead of tracking the runner behind her.
whyItMatters: Leaves a Red player unmarked in a dangerous central position.
coachingCue: See the ball, see the player.
microCorrection: Maintain a 'side-on' stance to keep both the ball and the opponent in view.
u10Adaptation: Keep one eye on the ball and one eye on your 'buddy'.

title: Passing Weight
whatHappened: The initial pass from the kickoff is too soft.
whyItMatters: Invites immediate pressure and risks an early turnover.
microCorrection: Follow through toward the target to ensure the ball reaches the teammate's feet.
u10Adaptation: Pass it like you're trying to break a paper wall.

title: Core Stability in Contact
whatHappened: A Navy player stumbles slightly after a shoulder-to-shoulder challenge.
whyItMatters: Loss of balance leads to loss of possession.
coachingCue: Be the hammer, not the nail.
microCorrection: Engage the core and lean into the contact with the shoulder.
u10Adaptation: Be strong like a superhero when someone bumps you.

title: Transition to Defense
whatHappened: When the ball is cleared, the Navy forwards stay high and don't help.
whyItMatters: Creates a massive gap between the lines for the opponent to exploit.
microCorrection: Forwards should drop to the halfway line when the ball is in their own third.
u10Adaptation: Everyone helps defend, even the strikers.

title: Goalkeeper Distribution
whatHappened: After a save/pickup, the keeper looks to punt rather than roll the ball.
whyItMatters: Punting is a 50/50; rolling to a defender builds controlled play.
microCorrection: Look for the wide defenders first before deciding to kick long.
u10Adaptation: Roll the ball to your friends like a bowling ball.

title: Sustained Sprinting
whatHappened: Ava maintains her speed for over 20 yards during the breakaway.
whyItMatters: Prevents the defenders from catching up and making a recovery tackle.
microCorrection: Maintain high knee drive even when tired at the end of the sprint.
u10Adaptation: Keep your engine running until the ball is in the net.

title: Pressure on the Ball
whatHappened: Navy players are 'containing' from 5 yards away rather than pressing.
whyItMatters: Gives the Red team too much time to look up and pass.
microCorrection: Sprint to within 2 yards, then slow down and get into a defensive stance.
u10Adaptation: Get close enough to smell their jersey, then stop.
